title: allium geyeri subsp. tenerum
intro: Allium geyeri subsp. tenerum, also known as Geyer's wild garlic or smallflower wild onion, is a subspecies of wild onion native to the western United States. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antibacterial and antiviral properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and combat infections. The health benefits of Allium geyeri subsp. tenerum include its ability to lower blood pressure, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. The plant can be used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
